sentence: O tempo não está nada bom hoje.
ipa: u ˈtẽpu ˈnɐ̃w̃ eˈsta ˈnadɐ ˈbõ ˈoʒi
gloss_en: The weather is not good at all today.
notes: Riverense: final /e/ raised to [i] in 'hoje', inherited from the urban-BP reference norm (carvalho1998_variation); nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'não' inherited from pt-BR (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: Estes sapatos custam quarenta pesos na feira.
ipa: ˈestis saˈpatus ˈkustɐ̃w̃ kwaˈɾẽtɐ ˈpezus nɐ ˈfejɾɐ
gloss_en: These shoes cost forty pesos at the market.
notes: Border lexicon 'pesos' (Uruguayan currency), the frontier vernacular of the Rivera region (sturza2021_territorio); alveolar coda /s/ [s] left inherited from pt-BR, not aspirated in the Portuguese (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: A consulta é amanhã às três da tarde.
ipa: ɐ kõˈsultɐ ˈɛ amaˈɲã as ˈtɾes dɐ ˈtaɾdi
gloss_en: The appointment is tomorrow at three in the afternoon.
notes: Coda /l/ retained as [l] in 'consulta' — no vocalisation to [w], aligning with the Spanish adstrate (carvalho1998_variation); dental /d/ before /i/ retained in 'tarde' → [ˈtaɾdi] (rona1965_fronterizo).

sentence: Meu irmão mora aqui perto, na fronteira.
ipa: ˈmew iˈɾmɐ̃w̃ ˈmɔɾɐ aˈki ˈpeɾtu nɐ fɾõˈtejɾɐ
gloss_en: My brother lives right here, on the border.
notes: Border-region reference to 'a fronteira' (Brazil–Uruguay frontier), the DPU heartland (sturza2021_territorio); nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'irmão' (elizaincin1992_contacto); retained [ej] in 'fronteira' (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: Segue reto e dobra na próxima esquina.
ipa: ˈseɡi ˈretu i ˈdobɾɐ nɐ ˈpɾɔʃimɐ eˈskinɐ
gloss_en: Go straight and turn at the next corner.
notes: Alveolar trill onset [r] in 'reto', aligning with border Spanish, not the guttural [ʁ] of urban BP (carvalho1998_variation); open-mid [ɔ] in 'próxima' inherited from pt-BR (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: A comida da minha mãe estava muito boa.
ipa: ɐ koˈmidɐ dɐ ˈmiɲɐ ˈmɐ̃j̃ eˈstavɐ ˈmujtu ˈboɐ
gloss_en: My mother's food was very good.
notes: Dental /d/ before /i/ retained in 'comida' — no affrication, kept under Spanish interference (carvalho1998_variation; hensey1972_border); palatal [ɲ] in 'minha', nasal diphthong [ɐ̃j̃] in 'mãe' (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: Onde é que tu deixaste a colher?
ipa: ˈõdi ˈɛ ki ˈtu deˈjʃasti ɐ koˈjeɾ
gloss_en: Where did you leave the spoon?
notes: Categorical vocalisation of the palatal lateral ⟨lh⟩ /ʎ/ → glide [j]: 'colher' → [koˈjeɾ], the single most salient Riverense marker (local 'coié' vs BP 'colher', carvalho1998_variation; rona1965_fronterizo); 'tu' 2sg preterite (hensey1972_border).

sentence: Quantos filhos tem aquela mulher?
ipa: ˈkwɐ̃tus ˈfijus ˈtẽj̃ aˈkelɐ muˈjeɾ
gloss_en: How many children does that woman have?
notes: ⟨lh⟩ → [j] in 'filhos' → [ˈfijos] and 'mulher' → [muˈjeɾ] (the /mujɛ/-type form of carvalho1998_variation; rona1965_fronterizo); final /s/ [s] inherited (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: Se tu quisesses, comprava aquela chácara.
ipa: si ˈtu kiˈzes̺is kõˈpɾavɐ aˈkelɐ ˈʃakaɾɐ
gloss_en: If you wanted, I would buy that little farm.
notes: 'tu' + 2sg imperfect subjunctive, the rural border paradigm (hensey1972_border); final /e/ raised to [i] in 'segue'-type context and 'se' → [si] (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: Tu compraste as frutas e as verduras na venda?
ipa: ˈtu kõˈpɾasti as ˈfɾutas i as veˈɾduɾas nɐ ˈvẽdɐ
gloss_en: Did you buy the fruit and the vegetables at the store?
notes: 'tu' 2sg 'compraste' (hensey1972_border); alveolar coda /s/ [s] across 'as frutas'/'as verduras', inherited from pt-BR (carvalho1998_variation); 'venda' country-store lexicon (sturza2021_territorio).

sentence: Amanhã nós vamos de carro até Rivera.
ipa: amaˈɲã ˈnɔs ˈvamus di ˈkaru aˈtɛ riˈveɾɐ
gloss_en: Tomorrow we are going by car to Rivera.
notes: Toponym Rivera, the Uruguayan border city and DPU centre (sturza2021_territorio); alveolar trill [r] in 'carro'/'Rivera' (carvalho1998_variation); palatal [ɲ] in 'amanhã' (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: Me liga quando tu chegares em casa.
ipa: mi ˈliɡɐ ˈkwɐ̃du ˈtu ʃeˈɡaɾis ẽj̃ ˈkazɐ
gloss_en: Call me when you get home.
notes: 'tu' + 2sg future subjunctive 'chegares' (hensey1972_border); final /e/ → [i] in 'me' → [mi] and 'liga' onset, the raised BR vocalism inherited (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: Bom dia, como é que tu andas de saúde?
ipa: ˈbõ ˈdiɐ ˈkomu ˈɛ ki ˈtu ˈɐ̃das di ˈsaudi
gloss_en: Good morning, how is your health doing?
notes: Dental /d/ before /i/ retained in 'dia' — no affrication (carvalho1998_variation; rona1965_fronterizo); 'tu andas' 2sg (hensey1972_border); nasal [õ] in 'bom' (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: Mais vale um pássaro na mão que dois voando.
ipa: ˈmajs ˈvali ˈũ ˈpas̺aɾu nɐ ˈmɐ̃w̃ ki ˈdojs voˈɐ̃du
gloss_en: A bird in the hand is worth two in the bush.
notes: Proverb in neutral register; nasal diphthong [ɐ̃w̃] in 'mão' inherited from pt-BR (elizaincin1992_contacto); alveolar coda /s/ [s] in 'mais'/'dois' (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: O carro do Ramón ficou na estrada de terra.
ipa: u ˈkaru du raˈmõ fiˈkow nɐ eˈstɾadɐ di ˈtɛrɐ
gloss_en: Ramón's car got stuck on the dirt road.
notes: Spanish first name Ramón, ordinary in the bilingual border community (sturza2021_territorio); alveolar trill [r] in 'carro'/'terra' and onset 'Ramón' (carvalho1998_variation); [ow] in 'ficou' (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: O leiteiro trouxe pouco leite outro dia.
ipa: u leˈjtejɾu ˈtɾowsi ˈpowku ˈlejti ˈowtɾu ˈdiɐ
gloss_en: The milkman brought little milk the other day.
notes: Retained falling diphthongs [ej]/[ow] in 'leiteiro'/'pouco'/'outro' (elizaincin1992_contacto); final /e/ raised to [i] in 'leite' → [ˈlejti] and 'dia', the inherited BR vocalism (carvalho1998_variation).

sentence: O sol forte queimava o milharal.
ipa: u ˈsɔl ˈfɔɾti keˈjmavɐ u mijaˈɾal
gloss_en: The strong sun scorched the cornfield.
notes: Coda /l/ retained as [l] in 'sol' → [ˈsol] and 'milharal' → [mijaˈɾal] — no [w] vocalisation, per the Spanish adstrate (carvalho1998_variation); ⟨lh⟩ → [j] in 'milharal' (rona1965_fronterizo).

sentence: A vizinha molhava as plantas de manhã.
ipa: ɐ viˈziɲɐ moˈjavɐ as ˈplɐ̃tas di maˈɲã
gloss_en: The neighbour watered the plants in the morning.
notes: Palatal [ɲ] in 'vizinha'/'manhã' (elizaincin1992_contacto); ⟨lh⟩ → [j] in 'molhava' → [moˈjavɐ], the Riverense lateral glide (carvalho1998_variation; rona1965_fronterizo).

sentence: O café estava ótimo, mas a sopa ficou fria.
ipa: u kaˈfɛ eˈstavɐ ˈɔtimu ˈmas ɐ ˈsopɐ fiˈkow ˈfɾiɐ
gloss_en: The coffee was great, but the soup came out cold.
notes: Open-mids [ɛ] ('café') and [ɔ] ('ótimo') inherited from the pt-BR seven-vowel system (carvalho1998_variation); [ow] in 'ficou' (elizaincin1992_contacto).

sentence: O velho trabalhava na lavoura com o filho.
ipa: u ˈveju tɾabaˈjavɐ nɐ laˈvowɾɐ kõ u ˈfiju
gloss_en: The old man worked in the field with his son.
notes: Threefold ⟨lh⟩ → [j]: 'velho' → [ˈveju], 'trabalhava' → [tɾabaˈjavɐ], 'filho' → [ˈfiju] — the categorical Riverense lateral-glide marker (carvalho1998_variation; rona1965_fronterizo); [ow] in 'lavoura' (elizaincin1992_contacto).
